1.error: marker 0.1437 / coord (19.2440, 47.5812) sits ~200 m before the apex (~19.2424, 47.5822) — looks like a braking point, not the apex; also it's a ~160° slow corner, not a true hairpin, scale 2 fits better than 1

2.error: scale 1 is wrong — T2 is a long medium-speed downhill left, not a hairpin; coord is ~170 m before the apex (~19.2482, 47.5812)

3.error: coord (19.2480, 47.5808) sits at T2's apex/exit, not at T3 — the right-hander apexes near (19.2476, 47.5825); marker 0.3007 looks ~0.03 early; also T3 is a fast uphill right, scale 4 not 3

4.error: T4 is a very fast left kink (one of the quickest corners on the track), scale 4 not 3; coord ~200 m before the kink at (~19.2505, 47.5862)

5.error: coord (19.2504, 47.5868) is on the approach straight — the long right apexes at the top of the track (~19.2506, 47.5884)

6.error: coord (19.2519, 47.5880) sits mid-T5 exit — the chicane is at (~19.2535, 47.5867); marker 0.5437 looks ~0.03 early

7.error: coord (19.2522, 47.5878) is before the actual chicane wiggle at (~19.2533, 47.5865)

9.error: coord (19.2537, 47.5855) sits at T8's exit — T9 apexes near (19.2557, 47.5846); T9 is a flowing medium right, scale 3 fits better than 2

10.error: coord (19.2557, 47.5845) is at T9's apex, not T10 — the left-hander is ~200 m further on at (~19.2556, 47.5827)

11.error: coord (19.2555, 47.5830) sits at T10's apex — T11 is at (~19.2565, 47.5814); also T11 is a very fast right, scale 4 not 2

12.error: coord (19.2547, 47.5799) is ~200 m up the straight before the corner at (~19.2530, 47.5786); T12 is a medium 90° right, scale 3

13.error: scale 1 too severe — T13 is a slow-medium ~120° left, not a hairpin; coord (19.2523, 47.5790) is well before the apex (~19.2502, 47.5796)

14.error: coord (19.2508, 47.5791) sits between T13 exit and T14 entry — apex is at (~19.2520, 47.5781); T14 is a long ~165° right onto the straight, scale 1-2 borderline, 2 preferred

gp.errors_summary: No structural issues (14 corners, all directions correct), but corner coordinates/markers are systematically ~0.02-0.04 (150-200 m) early — placed at braking/entry rather than apex, so several coords land on the previous corner's apex (T3→T2, T10→T9, T11→T10); several severity scales too tight (T2, T11, T13) or too soft (T3, T4). Hungaroring corners have no widely-used colloquial names — drivers use turn numbers, except the T6-T7 'Chicane'.
